chapter_context.summary: David and the leaders offer immense personal gold and silver gifts; the nation rejoices; Solomon is anointed king a second time, David dies in a ripe old age, and Solomon reigns.

outline.text: David's Generous Personal Gift of Gold and Silver
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David's Generous Personal Gift of Gold and Silver (29:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: The Leaders and Princes Give Willingly; Great National Rejoicing
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Leaders and Princes Give Willingly; Great National Rejoicing (29:6-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: David's Grand Thanksgiving Prayer; Solomon Anointed King Second Time
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David's Grand Thanksgiving Prayer; Solomon Anointed King Second Time (29:10-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: Solomon Established on David's Throne in Great Majesty
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Solomon Established on David's Throne in Great Majesty (29:23-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: David's Death and Burial After a Forty-Year Reign
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David's Death and Burial After a Forty-Year Reign (29:26-30), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: Covenant Lineage
key_themes.description: The meticulous genealogies show that God's plan is continuous and links post-exilic Israel back to creation.

key_themes.title: The Throne of David
key_themes.description: David's rise and worship preparations focus on establishing Jerusalem as the central theological hub of the kingdom.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 29, how does seeing God's faithfulness through history encourage you today?
How does this chapter prompt you to dedicate your personal plans and gifts to the LORD's work?

cultural_historical.title: The Post-Exilic Restoration
cultural_historical.text: Chronicles was written for the returning exiles to rebuild their religious identity, focusing heavily on priestly lineages and temple worship.
